project.name: dotfiles
project.description: Personal configuration files for Neovim, tmux, zsh, and AI coding tools

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore patterns for the dotfiles repository. Excludes Neovim plugin directories, local zsh overrides (secrets/host-specific config), Sisyphus agent working files, and vim-plug installed plugins.

nodes.name: .shellcheckrc
nodes.summary: ShellCheck linter configuration for the project. Forces bash interpretation since zsh is not natively supported, sets warning severity level, and disables sourcing checks for dynamic paths.

nodes.name: boulder.json
nodes.summary: Sisyphus boulder state configuration tracking active migration plan (vim-config-migration), session IDs, timestamps, and agent assignment (atlas). Used for AI agent workflow persistence.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: GNU Make build automation for dotfile management. Provides targets for running linters (shellcheck on zsh configs) and displaying help. Note: ShellCheck interprets zsh as bash which may produce false positives.

nodes.name: help
nodes.summary: Default make target that displays available commands: lint (all linters) and lint-shell (shellcheck on zsh configs).

nodes.name: lint
nodes.summary: Runs all linters by delegating to lint-shell target. Single dependency target for comprehensive linting.

nodes.name: lint-shell
nodes.summary: Executes shellcheck with bash interpretation on zsh config files (zsh/.zshenv, zsh/.zprofile, zsh/.zshrc). Suppresses errors to avoid breaking build on false positives from zsh-specific syntax.

nodes.name: LICENSE
nodes.summary: MIT License for the dotfiles repository. Copyright 2026 ekajaya740. Standard permissive open-source license allowing use, modification, and distribution with attribution.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Agent instructions documentation for AI agents working with this dotfiles repository. Defines workflow, safety rules, validation checklist, and Neovim plugin language server mappings.

nodes.name: KEYBINDINGS.md
nodes.summary: Complete keybindings reference for this dotfiles configuration covering tmux, Neovim, Vim, and zsh. Documents leader keys, pane/window management, LSP keybindings, and Oh My Zsh aliases.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project documentation describing repository layout, dependencies, installation instructions for multiple platforms (macOS, Arch Linux), and setup steps for Neovim/LazyVim, tmux, zsh with Oh My Zsh and Powerlevel10k.

nodes.name: .omc-config.json
nodes.summary: Oh-my-claudecode (OMC) configuration file with ultrawork execution mode, team settings (max 3 agents), and setup version 4.8.2.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code configuration and instructions for oh-my-claudecode (OMC) multi-agent orchestration layer. Defines operating principles, delegation rules, model routing, agent catalog, skills, and team pipeline.

nodes.name: init.lua
nodes.summary: Neovim entry point that sets leader keys (Space for mapleader and maplocalleader) and bootstraps LazyVim via require('config.lazy').

nodes.name: lazy-lock.json
nodes.summary: Lazy.nvim plugin lock file pinning 39 plugins including LazyVim core, telescope, nvim-tree, mason, LSP config, treesitter, catppuccin theme, git-blame, and various editing enhancements.

nodes.name: lazyvim.json
nodes.summary: LazyVim configuration file tracking extras, install version, and news state. Contains empty extras array and version metadata.

nodes.name: colorscheme.lua
nodes.summary: Colorscheme configuration that sets catppuccin-mocha theme with pcall error protection. Falls back gracefully if colorscheme not found.

nodes.name: keymaps.lua
nodes.summary: Comprehensive keymaps configuration including general keymaps, window management, tab navigation, plugin-specific bindings (nvim-tree, telescope, vim-maximizer), and LSP keybinds auto-attached on LspAttach.

nodes.name: set_file_picker_keymaps
nodes.summary: Configures file picker keymaps by clearing LazyVim defaults and setting custom nvim-tree and Snacks.explorer bindings. Scheduled and re-applied on VeryLazy and LazyVimStarted events.

nodes.name: lazy.lua
nodes.summary: Lazy.nvim bootstrap file that clones lazy.nvim plugin manager, sets up runtime path, requires config modules (options, keymaps, colorscheme), and configures LazyVim plugin specification.

nodes.name: options.lua
nodes.summary: Neovim options configuration setting line numbers, indentation (2-space tabs), search settings (smartcase), cursor line, appearance (dark theme, termguicolors), clipboard, and split window behavior.

nodes.name: autopairs.lua
nodes.summary: nvim-autopairs plugin configuration with treesitter integration. Disables autopairs in lua strings and javascript template strings, skips java.

nodes.name: comment.lua
nodes.summary: Comment.nvim plugin configuration with custom toggler keymaps (lc for line, bc for block) and ts_context_commentstring integration for JSX/TSX comment support.

nodes.name: git-blame.lua
nodes.summary: git-blame.nvim plugin configuration that enables inline git blame display. Simple setup with pcall protection for safe loading.

nodes.name: gitsigns.lua
nodes.summary: Gitsigns plugin configuration with safe import pattern. Shows git signs (add/change/delete) in the sign column.

nodes.name: indent-blankline.lua
nodes.summary: Indent guide configuration with optional rainbow lines support. Falls back to basic ibl setup if indent-rainbowline unavailable.

nodes.name: jdtls.lua
nodes.summary: Java Development Tools Language Server (jdtls) configuration module. Sets up Java LSP with Lombok support, workspace management, code generation templates, and JUnit/Mockito completion favorites.

nodes.name: setup
nodes.summary: Configures jdtls Java language server with Mason-installed path, Lombok agent, workspace directory, and comprehensive Java settings including formatting, code generation, imports organization, and static member favorites.

nodes.name: lualine.lua
nodes.summary: Minimal lualine statusline configuration. Uses default settings for the status bar display.

nodes.name: nvim-tree.lua
nodes.summary: File explorer configuration with netrw disabled. Sets up custom folder icons, filters for dotfiles/git-ignored files, and custom indent marker highlighting.

nodes.name: semantic-highlight.lua
nodes.summary: LSP semantic token highlighting configuration. Maps LSP semantic types (class, interface, enum, etc.) to Neovim highlight groups for consistent syntax coloring across themes.

nodes.name: tailwind-sorter.lua
nodes.summary: Minimal Tailwind CSS class sorter configuration. Uses default settings for automatic class ordering.

nodes.name: telescope-simulators.lua
nodes.summary: Configures telescope-simulators.nvim plugin for Android emulator and Apple simulator support. Sets up both simulator types and loads FZF extension.

nodes.name: telescope.lua
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Telescope fuzzy finder configuration with robust fallback handling for nvim-treesitter.configs module. Implements custom keybindings for navigation, quickfix, and path insertion. Configures pickers (find_files, live_grep, grep_string) with ripgrep backend and git file exclusions.

nodes.name: todo.lua
nodes.summary: Configures todo-comments.nvim plugin for highlighting TODO/FIXME comments. Maps leader+ft to TodoTelescope command.

nodes.name: treesitter.lua
nodes.summary: Configures nvim-treesitter with syntax highlighting, indentation, and autotag support. Defines ensure_installed list with 35+ language parsers including web, systems, and scripting languages. Sets up context-aware commentstring configuration for JavaScript/TypeScript.

nodes.name: wakatime.lua
nodes.summary: Configures vim-wakatime plugin for automatic time tracking. Simple setup with default configuration.

nodes.name: configs.lua
nodes.summary: Module providing fallback implementation for nvim-treesitter.configs API. Exposes setup, get_module, and is_enabled functions compatible with treesitter configuration interface. Designed to prevent errors when treesitter module is unavailable.

nodes.name: extras.lua
nodes.summary: Lazy.nvim plugin specification file declaring 25+ extra plugins beyond LazyVim defaults. Includes Catppuccin theme, alternative file tree (nvim-tree/neo-tree disabled), tmux navigation, git tools, telescope ecosystem, lsp enhancements, and development utilities. Each plugin references its config module.

nodes.name: live_grep.additional_args
nodes.summary: Returns additional ripgrep arguments for live_grep picker with hidden files and git exclusions

nodes.name: grep_string.additional_args
nodes.summary: Returns additional ripgrep arguments for grep_string picker with hidden files and git exclusions

nodes.name: formatting.lua
nodes.summary: Configures nvim-lint linters for JavaScript/TypeScript/Vue/Svelte/Shell/Ruby and disables conform.nvim formatter plugin

nodes.name: lsp.lua
nodes.summary: Major LSP configuration for 20+ language servers including TypeScript, Go, Rust, Python, Java, PHP, Ruby, Dart, Zig, Lua, and more. Includes autoformat on save, inlay hints configuration, and schema support via schemastore.nvim

nodes.name: mason.lua
nodes.summary: Mason.nvim configuration with ensure_installed list of 35+ LSP servers, formatters, linters, and debug adapters for comprehensive language support

nodes.name: snacks.lua
nodes.summary: Snacks.nvim picker configuration that disables default keybindings and enables hidden file search in files, grep, and explorer sources

nodes.name: oh-my-opencode.json
nodes.summary: OH-MY-OpenCode agent configuration defining 12 specialized agents (sisyphus, prometheus, oracle, etc.) with models from ollama-cloud, temperature settings, and fallback chains

nodes.name: opencode.json
nodes.summary: Main OpenCode configuration with plugins (oh-my-opencode, superpowers), providers (OpenAI GPT-5, Ollama, Claude), and MCP servers (GitHub, Notion, browser automation)

nodes.name: is_autoformat_enabled
nodes.summary: Helper function that checks if autoformat is enabled for a buffer by examining global vim.g.autoformat and buffer-local vim.b[bufnr].autoformat settings. Disables autoformat for HTML files.

nodes.name: rate-limit-fallback.json
nodes.summary: Rate limiting fallback configuration for OpenCode API with alternative models (ollama-cloud/glm-5, kimi-k2.5), cooldown period of 5 minutes, and pattern matching for rate limit errors

nodes.name: .tmux.conf
nodes.summary: Tmux configuration with custom keybindings for pane splitting/resizing, Vi copy mode, mouse support, TPM plugin manager, and plugins including vim-tmux-navigator, catppuccin theme, tmux-resurrect, and tmux-continuum for session persistence

nodes.name: plug.vim
nodes.summary: vim-plug plugin manager - vendored external dependency for managing Vim/Neovim plugins with commands like PlugInstall, PlugUpdate, PlugClean. MIT licensed by Junegunn Choi

nodes.name: .p10k.zsh
nodes.summary: Powerlevel10k theme configuration auto-generated by p10k configure wizard. Configures left/right prompt segments including os_icon, dir, vcs, status, command_execution_time, background_jobs, kubecontext, and various version managers. Uses rainbow powerline style with two-line prompt

nodes.name: .zprofile
nodes.summary: Zsh login shell configuration that starts X on login for Arch Linux (skipped for Omarchy/Wayland), manages ssh-agent daemon lifecycle with XDG_RUNTIME_DIR storage for socket persistence

nodes.name: .zshenv
nodes.summary: Zsh environment configuration sourced by all zsh sessions. Sets XDG directories, editor defaults, locale, history settings. Contains platform detection logic for Arch/Manjaro/Omarchy with environment variable exports for conditional config loading

nodes.name: .zshrc
nodes.summary: Main interactive zsh configuration. Enables p10k instant prompt, configures Oh My Zsh with plugins (git, z, docker, fzf, autosuggestions, syntax-highlighting), loads powerlevel10k theme based on platform detection, sets history options, defines aliases, configures fzf with fd/bat integration

nodes.name: dotfiles-zsh.md
nodes.summary: Markdown configuration file (22 lines)

nodes.name: vim-config-migration.md
nodes.summary: Markdown configuration file (119 lines)

nodes.name: zsh-omarchy.md
nodes.summary: Markdown configuration file (21 lines)

nodes.name: dotfiles-zsh.md
nodes.summary: Markdown configuration file (105 lines)

nodes.name: vim-config-migration.md
nodes.summary: Markdown configuration file (777 lines)

layers.name: Shell Layer
layers.description: Zsh configuration including environment variables, aliases, Oh My Zsh plugins, and Powerlevel10k theme. Manages shell behavior, PATH configuration, platform detection (Omarchy/Arch/macOS), and interactive shell customization.

layers.name: Editor Layer
layers.description: Neovim/Vim configuration using LazyVim framework. Includes LSP configuration, plugins for syntax highlighting (treesitter), completion (nvim-cmp), formatting, git integration, file navigation (nvim-tree, telescope), and language-specific support (Java/jdtls, semantic highlighting).

layers.name: Terminal Layer
layers.description: tmux terminal multiplexer configuration for session management, window/pane navigation, and persistent terminal sessions.

layers.name: AI Tools Layer
layers.description: Configuration for AI coding assistants including OpenCode (oh-my-opencode plugin orchestration, MCP servers, model providers) and Claude Code (CLAUDE.md instructions, OMC config). Enables multi-agent workflows and intelligent development assistance.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project documentation including README (setup instructions, dependencies, installation), AGENTS.md (automation guidelines for AI agents), KEYBINDINGS.md (custom shortcuts reference), and Sisyphus agent plans for task orchestration.

layers.name: Build/Automation Layer
layers.description: Build automation and quality tooling including Makefile for linting targets and .shellcheckrc for shell script quality configuration.

tour.title: Neovim Bootstrap
tour.description: Start at init.lua which sets leader keys and requires config.lazy to bootstrap LazyVim plugin manager. This is the entry point that loads the entire Neovim configuration.

tour.title: Plugin System & Extras
tour.description: Explore the extras.lua which defines additional plugins beyond LazyVim defaults, and discover the pattern of lazy-loading plugins with build steps and configuration callbacks.

tour.title: LSP Configuration - Multi-language Setup
tour.description: Deep dive into the LSP configuration with lsp.lua, mason.lua, and jdtls.lua. Understand how 20+ language servers are configured with specific settings, formatters, and the special Java jdtls setup with Lombok support.

tour.title: Zsh Configuration Chain
tour.description: Follow the zsh configuration flow from .zshenv (environment variables, platform detection) through .zshrc (Oh My Zsh, plugins, Powerlevel10k theme) to understand how shell startup works across macOS, Arch, and Omarchy.

tour.title: AI Tool Configurations
tour.description: Examine the OpenCode configuration with multiple LLM providers (OpenAI GPT-5, Ollama local/cloud) and the oh-my-opencode.json which defines specialized agents (Sisyphus, Prometheus, Atlas, etc.) with model fallbacks and category-based routing.

tour.title: Documentation & Agent Guidelines
tour.description: Review AGENTS.md for automation rules, symlink management with stow, validation procedures, and the extensive language server reference. Then study KEYBINDINGS.md for the complete keybinding reference across tmux, Neovim, and zsh.

tour.title: Integration Overview - How It All Ties Together
tour.description: Connect all the pieces: understand how stow creates symlinks from dotfiles/ to ~/.config, how Neovim's LSP auto-installs language servers via Mason, how Oh My Zsh plugins extend shell capabilities, and how tmux integrates with Neovim via vim-tmux-navigator for seamless pane navigation.
